title: The Best New Bars in Miami

title: Eating News in Miami, July 2026
summary: A different kind of tea time -- tipsy tea, an unbeatable offer for mango lovers, new high profile restaurant in Coral Gables, ladies night with all the perks, a new waterfront option for cocktails and delicious comfort food from leading chefs, decadent ice cream and churros,…
whatHappening: Miami's food and beverage scene is heating up with a wave of new dining and drinking experiences launching in July 2026. From tipsy tea events to waterfront cocktail bars and high-profile restaurant openings in Coral Gables, the city is rolling out diverse culinary offerings targeting different crowds.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering the expansion, which includes multiple restaurants and bars run by leading chefs and hospitality operators. These establishments are targeting various demographics including mango enthusiasts, women seeking ladies' night promotions, and cocktail-focused diners.
whyMatters: This dining boom signals Miami's continued evolution as a premier culinary destination competing with established food capitals. The diversity of offerings—from casual comfort food to upscale cocktail experiences—reflects growing consumer appetite for specialized food experiences and the city's economic vitality in the hospitality sector.
moreContext: Miami's food scene has undergone significant transformation over the past decade, with the city attracting internationally recognized chefs and investment in waterfront dining. The trend toward experiential dining events like tipsy tea and ladies' night promotions reflects broader industry shifts toward social-focused entertainment. Coral Gables, known for its Mediterranean Revival architecture and affluent demographics, continues to be a prime location for high-profile restaurant launches. Local publications like Miami Curated have become essential guides as the market becomes increasingly crowded with new concepts.

title: From Casual Lunch to Serious Dinner: Paperfish Sushi Brickell Stepped Up Its Game
summary: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has always been one of my “go to” places to have lunch or happy hour with a friend and they always like it. With all of the sushi restaurants in town you’d ask why Paperfish Sushi? The lovely décor that brightens up your day the moment you walk in,…
whatHappening: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has elevated its restaurant concept from a casual lunch and happy hour destination to a serious dinner establishment. The restaurant is leveraging its existing strengths—appealing décor and quality sushi—to attract an upscale dining clientele.
whoInvolved: Paperfish Sushi Brickell is a sushi restaurant operating in Miami's Brickell neighborhood, a commercial and residential district known for its upscale dining scene. The establishment has built a reputation as a reliable casual dining spot among local professionals and friends.
whyMatters: This repositioning allows Paperfish to compete in Miami's competitive fine dining sushi market and capture higher-margin dinner service revenue. The move reflects broader restaurant industry trends where establishments expand their daypart mix and price positioning to maximize profitability and reach affluent diners.
moreContext: Miami's Brickell district hosts numerous sushi restaurants, making differentiation crucial for success. Paperfish previously relied on its appealing interior design and consistent quality to build a loyal lunch and happy hour customer base. The shift toward dinner service requires the restaurant to enhance its offerings, ambiance, and service standards to justify premium pricing. This strategy allows the establishment to retain its casual daytime appeal while cultivating a more sophisticated evening experience.

title: New Places to Shop, Eat in the Miami Design District
summary: With the crowds having gone from the Dolce & Gabbana show and many from FIFA, now is a good time to make a visit to the Miami Design District, especially since new shops have opened that are brands that are probably new to you as well as restaurants. Plus, you might come across…
whatHappening: The Miami Design District is experiencing a retail renaissance with new shops and restaurants opening their doors. Post-event crowds from Dolce & Gabbana shows and FIFA are clearing, making it an ideal time for visitors to explore the neighborhood's fresh offerings.
whoInvolved: The Miami Design District, a premier shopping and dining destination in Miami, is welcoming new brands and food establishments. While specific brand names aren't detailed in the summary, the district attracts both local and international retailers and restaurateurs.
whyMatters: The influx of new venues strengthens the Design District's appeal as a retail and culinary destination, potentially driving foot traffic and economic activity in the neighborhood. For consumers, it means fresh shopping and dining experiences with brands and concepts that may be new to the Miami market.
moreContext: The Miami Design District has historically served as a hub for luxury shopping, design, and dining in South Florida. The timing of this expansion follows major events like Dolce & Gabbana fashion shows and FIFA activities, which typically draw significant crowds to Miami. The opening of new shops and restaurants suggests the district is capitalizing on its reputation and visitor traffic to attract additional merchants. This retail evolution reflects broader trends of established shopping districts adding curated, experiential venues to compete in the modern retail landscape.

title: PASTA: The Model Miami Restaurant
summary: Very reasonable prices, excellent food across the board, very good service, low noise decibel so you can easily talk, PASTA is a dream restaurant which should serve as a model to others in Miami. I'll even go so far as to say it has the best pasta in Miami. And on top of all…
whatHappening: PASTA restaurant in Miami is being praised as a model establishment that other restaurants should emulate. The venue is earning recognition for combining excellent food quality, reasonable pricing, and strong service standards.
whoInvolved: PASTA is a Miami-based restaurant that has caught the attention of Miami Curated, a local dining publication. The establishment operates as an independent dining venue focused on pasta cuisine.
whyMatters: In a competitive Miami dining market known for high prices and variable quality, PASTA demonstrates that a restaurant can deliver premium food and service without premium markups. This model could influence how other Miami restaurants approach pricing and operational standards, potentially shifting customer expectations across the market.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene has long been characterized by upscale establishments with correspondingly high price points, making affordable quality dining less common. PASTA distinguishes itself through multiple factors: it maintains low ambient noise levels allowing for conversation, delivers consistently high-quality food across its menu, and employs attentive service staff. The restaurant's pasta offerings are being positioned as the best in Miami, suggesting significant execution in its core offering. This recognition comes as consumers increasingly seek value without sacrificing experience quality.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour, a neighborhood on Miami Beach's bay side, is establishing itself as a premier destination that prioritizes wellness and local culture over tourist chaos. The area is gaining recognition as one of South Beach's most walkable and vibrant communities.
whoInvolved: Sunset Harbour residents and local businesses are driving the neighborhood's transformation into a wellness-focused destination. Miami Curated, which reported on the area, serves as a media voice highlighting Miami's lifestyle and cultural trends.
whyMatters: This shift reflects a broader trend of travelers and locals seeking authentic, health-conscious experiences over mass-tourism destinations. The rise of Sunset Harbour as a lifestyle hub could reshape South Beach's identity and attract a different demographic of visitors and residents prioritizing quality of life.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour distinguishes itself by its location on the quieter bay side of South Beach, contrasting sharply with the chaotic, party-centric energy of Ocean Drive. The neighborhood emphasizes a chic, wellness-oriented lifestyle that appeals to locals and conscious travelers. This positioning reflects post-pandemic consumer preferences for walkable neighborhoods with local amenities and cultural authenticity rather than traditional tourist attractions.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant promotion month, is returning this July with 75 participating establishments. The lineup represents the program's largest roster to date, featuring everything from Michelin-recognized fine dining to casual neighborhood spots across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with over 75 restaurants ranging from upscale destinations to local favorites throughout the community. The participating venues span multiple culinary styles and price points across the city.
whyMatters: The expanded event drives foot traffic and revenue to local restaurants during the traditionally slower summer season while giving diners access to premium dining experiences at promotional prices. For Coral Gables' hospitality sector, this signals strong economic confidence and positions the city as a destination dining hub in the Miami area.
moreContext: Taste the Gables has become an annual fixture that capitalizes on summer dining promotions, similar to other major cities' restaurant weeks. The growth from previous years to 75 participants demonstrates increasing restaurant interest and community support for the initiative. July timing targets both locals and summer visitors, extending the tourist season's economic benefits. The inclusion of Michelin-recognized establishments alongside neighborhood gems reflects Coral Gables' positioning as both an upscale dining destination and a community-focused neighborhood.
